data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism describes a 'migratory domino effect' moving sequentially from west to east or north to south, yet the scope includes Cyprus while excluding Assyria. Geographically, a north-to-south or west-to-east migration pressure originating in the Aegean/Anatolia would logically impact Assyria (to the east of the Hittites and Levant) before or concurrently with Cyprus. By excluding Assyria—which historically remained robust during this period—the model implicitly admits the 'domino' chain was broken or halted before reaching the easternmost targets, undermining the claim of a continuous, unstoppable migratory consumption of resources that only Egypt managed to halt.
